identifying_variation: policy counterfactuals that change enforcement, registration costs, and payroll costs
observed_margin: registration, payroll reporting, hidden employment, productivity, firm size
diagnostic_caution: Separate firm registration from payroll reporting and hidden employment.

identifying_variation: estimated search equilibrium matching workers and firms across formal and informal jobs
diagnostic_caution: Do not interpret wage gaps without sorting, benefits, and search frictions.

identifying_variation: increase in the expected cost of informal employment
observed_margin: formal employment, payroll reporting, wages, firm response
diagnostic_caution: Check whether the observed response is formalization, employment loss, or reclassification.

identifying_variation: health insurance eligibility discontinuity around formal status
observed_margin: formalization, insurance take-up, labor supply, welfare
diagnostic_caution: Name the benefit, tax, or eligibility margin before drawing welfare conclusions.

identifying_variation: historical legal change in coercive contract enforcement
observed_margin: wages, labor mobility, sensitivity to labor demand
diagnostic_caution: State whose claims became enforceable and how mobility or bargaining changed.

identifying_variation: observed transitions across formal work, informal work, self-employment, and unemployment
observed_margin: transition hazards, wage changes, benefit changes
diagnostic_caution: Transitions identify mobility patterns but may not isolate firm compliance.

identifying_variation: linked firm registry, payroll records, and audit exposure
observed_margin: reported employment, hidden employment proxy, wages, separations
diagnostic_caution: Linked records help, but missing informal firms can still bias the reallocation picture.
